title: DSR n_trials: deflate by family-wise trial count, not per-primary
source: LdP book audit (6 parallel read-only auditors vs AFML + Machine Learning for Asset Managers, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'audita el proyecto usando los libros'.
why: Deflated Sharpe Ratio uses n_trials = per-fold per-trade Sharpes across the 4 models WITHIN ONE PRIMARY (run_xau_d1.py:623-626, n_trials_global=len(sr_trials) at :661). The real search space is larger: 2 primaries x 4 models x 7-point threshold grid, plus every backlog config iteration. AFML 11/13 (AFML_book.txt:4160) requires deflating by the FULL number of trials carried out. Per-primary scoping UNDERSTATES trials and INFLATES DSR significance for any survivor. (Note: the per-fold-as-trial choice inflates trial variance in the conservative direction, but the cross-primary undercount dominates.)
value_rationale: Correctness of the headline overfit-control statistic; prevents over-claiming significance at the promotion gate. Complements B0121/B0122.
scope: Feed DSR a run-wide trial count spanning all primaries x models x thresholds (and ideally the backlog trial ledger AFML 11 advises)
OR document dsr as an explicit per-primary LOWER BOUND on deflation
Reconcile units: sr_observed (pooled-across-folds) vs sr_trials elements (per-fold) -- put them on matched footing
recommendation: Deflate by family-wise trial count; until then label DSR a per-primary lower bound.
out_of_scope: PBO gate (B0122), MinTRL stop rule (B0121), CPCV multi-path (B0123).

external_refs.label: AFML 11/13 deflate by number of trials

history.reason: LdP book audit: DSR trial pool scoped to single primary, undercounts family-wise trials

Implemented: deflated_sharpe_ratio gains n_trials override (variance from empirical pool, E[max] from family-wise count = models x folds x threshold-grid); psr_dsr.json records n_trials_familywise as per-primary lower bound. Both orchestrators.
fix: LÃ³pez-de-Prado audit fixes B0129-B0133 (purge, MDA, CFI, DSR, calib)
verification: DSR N should be effective-independent count (B0143); formulas confirmed byte-exact vs LdP DSR.py/PSR.py
test(regression): re-anchor Phase 1 baseline to post-audit-fix numbers (B0129-B0133)
